title: Setting up a liquid culture and harvesting C.elegans
author: Vidur Sabharwal
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xcpfivn
materials: Aspirate most of the liquid from the flask.

steps.title: Add 30ml S Medium to a sterilized 250 ml flask
steps.instruction: Add 30ml S Medium to a sterilized 250 ml flask.

steps.title: Inoculate the S Medium with a concentrated E
steps.instruction: Inoculate the S Medium with a concentrated E. coli OP50 pellet to a final concentration of 30g/L

steps.title: Wash each of 5 large plates (90cm) of C
steps.instruction: Wash each of 5 large plates (90cm) of C. elegans (just cleared of bacteria) with 5 ml S Medium and add to the 30 ml flask.

steps.title: 84:00:00 20 °C Put the flask...
steps.instruction: 84:00:00 20 °C Put the flask on a shaker at 200rpm

steps.title: Put the flask on ice to...
steps.instruction: Put the flask on ice to allow the worms to settle. 00:15:00

steps.title: Transfer the remaining liquid to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the remaining liquid to a 15 ml borosil conical tube using a glass pasteur pipette and spin for at least 2 min at 500 × g to pellet the worms. Young larvae may take longer than 2 min to pellet. 00:02:00

steps.title: Aspirate the remaining liquid and wash...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the remaining liquid and wash with 10x volume of M9 twice. Spin for at least 2 min at 500 × g to pellet the worms

steps.title: Transfer the worms to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the worms to a 1.5ml eppendorf, incubate on ice for 10 mins 00:10:00 and remove as much supernatant as possible.

steps.title: Add IP Buffer to the Pellet...
steps.instruction: Add IP Buffer to the Pellet with 2 parts homogenization buffer : 1 part worm pellet and store at -80C for upto 3 months

steps.title: Transfer 20 L4 hermaphrodites each to 10 90cm plates
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 L4 hermaphrodites each to 10 90cm plates. They will starve out in approximately 6 days. Start the following steps on the day the plates are almost starved. 20 °C 144:00:00

steps.title: After the second wash step, animals...
steps.instruction: After the second wash step, animals were resuspended in 20 ml of ice-cold M9 lacking MgSO4, followed by the addition of 20 ml of ice-cold 60 % sucrose in H2O.

steps.title: After vigorous mixing of the sucrose/worm...
steps.instruction: After vigorous mixing of the sucrose/worm mixture, 4 ml of ice-cold M9 lacking MgSO4 was gently layered on top, and the worms were centrifuged at 400 g for 2 min at 4 °C. The sucrose float steps were performed as quickly as possible, as otherwise, the layer of animals failed to form properly. A layer of animals was now visible on top of the sucrose, while contaminants sedimented at the bottom.

steps.title: To maximize recovery, 30 ml of...
steps.instruction: To maximize recovery, 30 ml of supernatant was aspirated from the sucrose float, and distributed into four 50 ml tubes that were subsequently filled by addition of room-temperature M9 lacking MgSO4. The room temperature M9 allowed the animals to digest OP50 bacteria in their intestine.

steps.title: The four tubes were placed on...
steps.instruction: The four tubes were placed on ice to cool down for 30 min, after which the animals were washed twice in M9 buffer. Add IP Buffer to the Pellet with 2 parts homogenization buffer : 1 part worm pellet and store at -80C for upto 3 months
